store_header.notes: n=116. Address/phone lines are body-identical (cap_rel med 1.01, stroke_rel med 1.02). The VONS logo is a separate sub-style: 27/116 lines tier=large, cap_rel med 4.11x body (range 3.26-4.66), reverse-video (white-on-black) on 17/116 lines. Render logo as its own element, not scaled text. The 1 underline hit is a vons.com URL line, likely a coupon-zone artifact.

savings.notes: n=84. Two sub-weights: dept 'Price You Pay' header lines are bold (30/84 tier=bold, stroke_rel med 1.42); Member Savings / YOUR SAVINGS value lines are lighter but still above body (stroke_rel med 1.15). Section-wide stroke_rel med 1.26, cap_rel med 1.04. Same glyph size as body; emphasis is stroke weight only.

section_header.notes: n=23. Dept banners (PRODUCE, MEAT, REFRIG/FROZEN, LIQUOR, GEN MERCHANDISE): stroke_rel med 1.34 (p25 1.18, p90 1.43), 15/23 tier=bold. cap_rel med 1.05, i.e. bold at body size, not enlarged.

total_line.notes: n=66, heterogeneous. '**** BALANCE' lines are body weight (stroke_rel med 1.03, n=26) with slight size bump (cap_rel med 1.08). 'TOTAL NUMBER OF ITEMS SOLD' is plain body (stroke_rel med 1.00, n=26). The savings-block 'Total X.XX' line IS bold (stroke_rel med 1.31, up to 1.61, n=14) — style that one line bold, not BALANCE.

summary.notes: n=81. TAX/CHANGE/Credit Purchase lines: cap_rel med 1.04, stroke_rel med 0.97, 80/81 tier=normal. Plain body.

payment.notes: n=108. cap_rel med 1.04, stroke_rel med 0.99. Plain body. Card-slip fields that print reverse-video/boxed would be invisible to this scan (see limitations).

points.notes: n=20 (small sample). cap_rel med 1.03, stroke_rel med 1.01. Plain body; occasional promo line measured large (1/20).

footer.notes: n=28. Tightest match to body: cap_rel med 1.00 (p25 0.94, p90 1.04), stroke_rel med 1.00. 28/28 tier=normal.

item.notes: n=30 explicitly tagged 'item'; the 406 'other' lines behave identically (cap_rel med 1.00, stroke_rel med 1.00). This IS the body baseline: sizeScale/weight 1.0 by construction. Underlines in 'other' (13/406) are coupon/ad fine print ('5% OFF', 'stock'), not receipt body.

S.note: curved caps measure heavy; likely real ink gain on curves

-.note: horizontal-bar glyph; stroke estimator reads bar thickness high

=.note: double-bar; almost certainly a measurement artifact (bars merge), do not thicken

z.note: diagonal-dominant; known diagonal measurement bias, treat with caution

N.note: diagonal reads thin; measurement bias suspected

limitations: Reverse-video / boxed fields are OCR-invisible: this scan is word-driven, so any region OCR failed to read (solid black banners, boxed card-slip fields) produced no line at all. Observed reverse_video rates (17/116 store_header logo, 7/406 other, 0 elsewhere) are LOWER BOUNDS, not true zeros — Costco taught us this. Do not conclude Vons has no reverse-video fields outside the logo.
1 of 30 receipts (129ee4aa_1) had no body baseline (body_cap_px null); its lines are excluded from normalized percentiles but counted in underline/reverse-video rates.
39 lines had null cap_px and 5 null stroke_med; excluded from the respective percentile pools.
Diagonal-dominant glyphs (z, Z, x, N, /) show known stroke-measurement bias in this pipeline; their letterOverrides describe measurements, not necessarily true ink weight.
'=' strokeScale 1.64 is a double-bar merge artifact; '-' 1.19 is bar-thickness vs stem-thickness comparison, not boldness.
section_header (n=23) and points (n=20) samples are small; percentiles are coarse.
The savings-block bold 'Total' line only appears on receipts with club-card savings (14/30 receipts); its bold verdict rests on n=14 lines.
